description_long: This is a vintage Soviet-era soda vending machine, primarily constructed from metal and painted in a worn, faded red color. The front features a rectangular display panel with Cyrillic text, a coin slot, two small rectangular buttons, and a large recessed product retrieval slot below. The machine's surface is heavily weathered, showing signs of age and use, with patches of exposed metal and scuff marks. The sides and back are plain, with no visible openings or features, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The overall shape is a tall, rectangular box with a slightly sloped top, typical of mid-20th-century vending machines. The machine stands on small feet, elevating it slightly above the ground.
description: A weathered, red Soviet-era soda vending machine with a Cyrillic display, coin slot, buttons, and a large retrieval slot on the front.
description_view_0: The front view shows the display panel with Cyrillic text, coin slot, two buttons, and a large product retrieval slot.
description_view_1: The left side view is plain, showing only the worn red metal surface with no additional features.
description_view_2: The back view is featureless, displaying a flat, weathered red metal surface.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, with a plain, worn red metal surface and no visible openings.
attribute_display_panel: A rectangular display panel is located at the top front of the machine, featuring printed Cyrillic text in red and white. It is flat, wide, and occupies the upper section above the controls.
attribute_payment_interface: A coin slot is visible on the front, just below the display panel and above the product retrieval slot.
attribute_product_selection_buttons: Two small rectangular buttons are present on the front, symmetrically placed below the display panel.
attribute_product_retrieval_slot: A large, rectangular recessed slot is located on the lower front, designed for retrieving dispensed products.
attribute_branding_or_signage: Branding consists of a large sign with Cyrillic text in red and white on the upper front display panel.
